_meta.purpose: single machine-readable index queried by choose_graph.py so styles/rules are stored once, not searched
_meta.sources: styles from graph-style-library.md; anti-patterns from FriendsDontLetFriends (MIT, DOIs cited); journal palettes hex from ggsci/ggprism (GPL-3 code NOT used, hex are facts); style engines from SciencePlots + ultraplot (MIT); stat methods from statistics-rigor.md + domain-conventions.md
_meta.scope_rule: GROUP-8 styles + modeling stats are hard-scoped to in-silico/modeling/signal deliverables; never launder onto wet-lab dissolution/PK/BE/biological endpoints | S46-S62 added 2026-07-19 from user-supplied reference figures (Ex1): each carries its own domain_fit/requires; the omics/cytometry family (S49-S54) is scoped to omics/single-cell/cytometry deliverables and must not be laundered onto dissolution/PK/BE endpoints. | S69-S74 added 2026-07-19 from the ggplot2-20-journal-cases collection (14 of 20 were already covered; these 6 were the genuinely-new idioms). Each carries its own domain_fit; swimmer/CLD/binned-heatmap/Mantel/polar-heatmap are hard-scoped to their fields and must not launder onto dissolution/PK/BE endpoints (CLD needs a real post-hoc; a binned ratio must centre on its threshold).

styles.name: Bar + overlaid individual data points (dot-on-bar), mean ± s.d
styles.note: dot-on-bar mitigates AP1

styles.name: Jittered strip / beeswarm (points only, no bar)
Violin / box-and-whisker / raincloud
styles.note: AP2: violin/box unstable at small n (quartiles stabilize ~n>50); never at n<20

styles.name: Overlaid distribution / spectral trace (intensity vs size/wavelength)
Large-N screening bar array (the "library screen")
styles.note: whole library incl. losers (anti-cherry-pick)

styles.name: Finalist bar + pairwise significance brackets
requires: named test + exact P + n.s. shown

styles.name: Grouped / clustered bar (two-factor: condition × category)
Dose–response bar ladder (ascending/descending staircase)
Dual-axis paired bar (two linked properties of one sample)
Floating / range bar (min→max with mean line) for DoE
Normalized bar with a dashed reference line (= 1 or = control)
Dose–response / concentration–response line with fit
Observed-vs-predicted parity plot (y = x line)
Bland–Altman (difference vs mean) agreement plot
Correlation / regression scatter with CI band
Levy plot (in-vivo time vs in-vitro time)
Diverging log2 fold-change heatmap (overview) + promoted bars (detail)
requires: reorder rows/cols (AP5)
cap outliers ~95pct (AP6)
styles.note: diverging ONLY for signed fold-change (AP3)

styles.name: Tornado / forest sensitivity plot
Loss / convergence curve (training + validation)
Grid-convergence / mesh-independence plot (GCI)
Error / uncertainty band on a time-course (CI or s.d. ribbon)
Chemical-structure grid / R-group library plate (small-multiples of molecules)
Micrograph with scale bar (cryo-TEM / EM / confocal)
AUC / summary-scalar bar (collapse a curve to one number)
styles.note: the honest 'parts/whole & scalar' answer instead of pie/donut (AP10/AP11)

styles.name: Confidence-interval / equivalence forest (ratio vs bounds)
Sankey / alluvial / flow diagram
Inline data table as a figure panel (DoE / lookup)
Multi-series kinetic / time-course line (mean ± s.d.)
Dissolution / release profile overlay (% released vs time)
Semi-log PK concentration–time profile
styles.note: semi-log, geometric mean, 90% CI

styles.name: Kaplan–Meier survival / time-to-event step curve + log-rank
styles.note: never an endpoint bar

styles.name: IVIS / spatial heat-overlay image grid (shared calibrated colorbar)
Flow-cytometry density / dot plot with on-plot % gate
Flow-histogram overlay stack (offset density curves per marker)
ROC / PR curve
Confusion matrix / calibration plot
Multichannel IF overlay + small-multiples micrograph matrix
3D solution-field surface (`plot_surface`) with view-angle control
styles.note: orientation only; not a substitute for GCI/parity

styles.name: Waterfall / stacked-offset profile plot
2D field / matrix heatmap (`pcolormesh` / `imshow`) + calibrated colorbar
requires: sequential CB-safe map (no jet, AP12)

styles.name: Spectrogram / time-frequency (Gabor / STFT)
styles.note: state window length

styles.name: Stem plot — discrete / sparse spectrum
Singular-value (scree) spectrum + cumulative-variance
PCA / low-dimensional projection (score plot) + variance-explained
Phase portrait / state-space trajectory
Paired before/after plot with per-subject connecting lines

domain_fit.PK: crossover/BE data are paired by subject — pair the points AND analyse on the log scale (S25/S30)
domain_fit.BE: required: pairing is the design; an unpaired test discards the blocking

requires: draw every subject's own line
analyse with a PAIRED test (paired t / Wilcoxon signed-rank)
styles.note: if the design is paired, hiding the pairing is a DS2 error — two independent-looking boxes overstate variance

styles.name: Dot plot against shaded reference / threshold zones

domain_fit.PK: therapeutic window / trough target bands — state the source of the band
domain_fit.PBPK: predicted exposure against a named safety threshold

requires: label each zone and cite where the threshold comes from
styles.note: the bands turn a bare ranking into an interpretable call (below/within/above target)

styles.name: Broken-axis dot plot for a long-tailed count
requires: dots only — never break the axis of a BAR chart (AP9: length must encode value)
mark the break on both facing spines
styles.note: legitimate only for position-based marks; consider a log axis first

styles.name: Dual-encoded dot-plot matrix (dot size = fraction, dot colour = level)
requires: a size legend AND a colorbar — two encodings need two keys
state what the size fraction is a fraction OF
styles.note: carries two statistics per cell where a heatmap carries one; do not use if only one statistic exists

styles.name: Matrix heatmap with categorical annotation tracks
requires: order samples by the annotation so the block structure is visible
diverging scale ONLY if the matrix is genuinely signed (z-score); sequential otherwise (AP3)
styles.note: the tracks let a reader see whether the pattern follows the biology or a batch/confounder

styles.name: Enrichment dot plot (ratio on x, count as size, FDR as colour)
requires: adjusted p (BH/FDR) across ALL sets tested, not just the ones plotted
state the background/universe size
styles.note: strictly more informative than an enrichment bar chart, which encodes one quantity

styles.name: GSEA running-enrichment plot (ES curve + member rug + ranked metric)
requires: NES + FDR from a real permutation null over a FAMILY of sets (an FDR over one set is meaningless)
shared x across all three tiers
styles.note: answers 'is this set concentrated at one end of the ranked list', which an enrichment bar cannot

styles.name: Embedding + feature-plot couplet (clusters, then the same coordinates by one feature)
requires: identical coordinates in both panels
direct on-plot cluster labels instead of a long legend where it fits
styles.note: a separated embedding is a hypothesis, not a test — back a class claim with a held-out classifier (S35/S36)

styles.name: Sequential gating-strategy panel row (density + gate + % of parent)
requires: % of PARENT at every step and the final % of total
each panel shows only the population the previous gate selected
styles.note: the raw gate behind a reported percentage — a bare % with no gating panel is unauditable

styles.name: Multi-stage alluvial / cohort flow with proportional ribbons
requires: mass must be conserved across stages — the widths must sum
label the n at every node
styles.note: use for a cohort moving through sequential decisions; a single split is just a stacked bar

styles.name: Radial dendrogram with value-scaled tip markers
requires: state the linkage + distance metric
a size legend for the tip encoding
styles.note: maps a measured value onto a FIXED tree; use a rectangular dendrogram (D05) when the tree itself is the result

styles.name: Network with detected communities + labelled hubs
requires: name the layout AND its seed (AP8: appearance drives interpretation)
name the community-detection algorithm
label hubs only, not every node
styles.note: try more than one layout before believing the structure you see

styles.name: Study-design / dosing-timeline schematic

domain_fit.PK: sampling schedule + dosing events must be legible enough to reproduce the study
domain_fit.dissolution: state medium, apparatus and sampling times on the schematic

requires: a real time axis in real units
every arm, dose event and readout labelled
styles.note: this is the M2 'panel a = orientation' figure; a results figure that precedes its design schematic is harder to review

styles.name: Analysis / model pipeline block schematic

domain_fit.PINN: show inputs, the loss terms and the validation split — a pipeline box diagram is not a substitute for loss curves + parity (S19/S13)
domain_fit.CFD: show the solver chain and where validation enters

requires: every box is a real step someone could re-run
styles.note: orientation only — it never substitutes for the convergence/parity evidence a modelling claim owes

styles.name: Offset trace array with a scale bar (no y-axis)
requires: a scale bar carrying BOTH units when the y-axis is dropped
mark stimulus/event times
styles.note: for many simultaneous traces where relative timing is the point; use small-multiples if absolute values must be compared

styles.name: Parity plot with bidirectional (x and y) error bars + flagged outliers

domain_fit.IVIVC: required alongside %PE (<10% internal) + leave-one-formulation-out; a bare R2 is a DS5 blocker
domain_fit.PINN: pair with relative-L2 and the loss curves (S19)

requires: identity line
a QUANTIFIED agreement metric on the panel — never 'agrees well'
styles.note: use when BOTH axes carry measurement error; ordinary parity (S13) understates disagreement then

styles.name: Grouped regression with pooled + per-group fits (Simpson check)
requires: report the pooled r AND every within-group r
state n per group
styles.note: guards against Simpson's paradox — a pooled correlation can reverse within groups; never report only the pooled fit for grouped data

styles.name: Size distribution with percentile readout (D10/D50/D90 + span)

domain_fit.dissolution: particle size drives dissolution rate — report the distribution, not just a mean
domain_fit.PK: size governs biodistribution; state the weighting (volume vs intensity vs number)

requires: percentiles COMPUTED from the cumulative curve, never typed in
state the weighting (Q3 volume / Q0 number) and the technique
styles.note: a Z-average alone hides bimodality and tails; the span (D90-D10)/D50 is the width statistic reviewers look for

styles.name: Quantified overlap of two distributions (overlap coefficient)

domain_fit.PK: exposure overlap between arms is not bioequivalence — BE needs S25 (90% CI vs 80-125%)

requires: label the shaded area as exactly the statistic you computed (OVL = the integral of min(f1,f2))
give a magnitude too (AUC or Cohen's d), not just the picture
styles.note: answers 'how separable are these groups', which two means +/- SD cannot; shade the intersection and mark the crossing threshold

styles.name: Annotated spectrum with assigned bands

domain_fit.formulation: FTIR/Raman evidence of encapsulation or polymorph change — assign the bands you rely on

requires: DETECT peak positions from the array so labels cannot drift from the data
real superscript units via mathtext (cm$^{-1}$), never a literal caret
keep the conventional axis direction (FTIR runs high to low wavenumber)
styles.note: an unassigned spectrum proves nothing; the assignment is the argument

styles.name: PCA score plot with per-group confidence ellipses
requires: run a REAL decomposition and print % variance per axis from the singular values
state the ellipse definition (95%, chi-square 2 df) and that it is a covariance ellipse, not a CI of the mean
styles.note: ellipses show group dispersion and overlap that bare scores hide; separation is still a hypothesis, not a test (back with S35/S36)

styles.name: Route-of-administration / biodistribution schematic

domain_fit.PK: organ shares must be %ID (or %ID/g — say which) and account for the blood pool
domain_fit.PBPK: the compartments drawn should match the compartments modelled

requires: carry a real quantity per organ so the cartoon is data-bearing, not decorative
the shares plus the unrecovered fraction must account for the dose
styles.note: the M2 orientation panel for an in-vivo study; a purely decorative organ cartoon earns nothing

styles.name: Mechanism cartoon (binding to uptake pathway), numbered steps

domain_fit.formulation: the proposed uptake route should match the evidence panels that follow (inhibitor/temperature blocks)

requires: every step named in the caption must be DRAWN on the canvas
elements described as touching must actually touch — check the geometry, do not eyeball it
number the steps so the caption can reference them
styles.note: orientation only: a mechanism cartoon is a hypothesis diagram and never substitutes for the experiment that tests it

styles.name: Swimmer plot (per-subject response timeline)

domain_fit.PK: works as a per-formulation stability/exposure timeline; state what the bar length measures
domain_fit.clinical: one bar per subject; response category by colour, on/off-study events as glyphs

requires: one row per subject, sorted by duration
a legend for BOTH the response colours AND the event glyphs
styles.note: shows individual trajectories and durability at a glance, which a Kaplan-Meier (S2) aggregates away; pair them

styles.name: Grouped bars with compact-letter-display (CLD) significance

domain_fit.dissolution: do NOT letter single-timepoint % as a profile claim — that needs f2 (S27); letters are for endpoint means only
domain_fit.PK: letter geometric means on the log scale, not linear mean±SD (S30)

requires: show the replicate points on the bars (AP1)
state the post-hoc test the letters come from (e.g. Tukey HSD); bars sharing a letter are not significantly different
styles.note: the compact-letter-display reports ALL pairwise outcomes at once, where a few brackets (S1) show only selected pairs; needs a real post-hoc, not eyeballed letters

styles.name: Binned-colour heatmap with row-metadata table (antibiogram)

domain_fit.PK: fold-change vs a reference (RR, ratio-to-RLD) binned onto interpretable thresholds reads better than a continuous ramp

requires: a stepped/segmented legend (BoundaryNorm), NOT a continuous colorbar
bins centred on the meaningful threshold (e.g. 1 for a ratio); diverging only because the ratio is signed around it
styles.note: binning maps a continuous value onto decision categories (susceptible/resistant, within/out of spec); use a continuous heatmap (I03/J06) when the gradient itself is the message

styles.name: Circos / circular ideogram with ribbon links
requires: links dip toward the centre (Bezier), never straight chords across the face
colour links by a categorical driver with a legend; keep rim labels from colliding
styles.note: for many pairwise links around one cyclic/positional axis (genomic loci, migration flows); a bipartite graph (E04) or alluvial (S55) is clearer when the axis is not cyclic

styles.name: Mantel composite (correlation heatmap + linked test edges)
requires: encode the test statistic by width, its p by colour, its sign by solid/dashed — three separate legends
a real Mantel/partial-Mantel needs distance matrices + a permutation p; do not label an ordinary correlation as Mantel
styles.note: the microbiome/ecology idiom relating an environment-variable correlation structure to community matrices; overkill for a single pairwise question (use S13/S14)

styles.name: Polar (radial) heatmap — concentric rings x angular sectors
requires: keep the printed cell values upright and legible
diverging scale only if the value is genuinely signed (partial r); sequential otherwise (AP3)
styles.note: a heatmap wrapped to polar reads compactly when sectors are a natural cycle or a long categorical list; a rectangular heatmap (I03) is more accurate for precise magnitude comparison — angle/area distort. Distinct from a polar rose (H09)

antipatterns.name: Bars for means separation (bars hide distribution)
antipatterns.fix: show the distribution: dot-on-bar / strip / box

antipatterns.name: Violin/box for small n
antipatterns.fix: quartiles unstable at small n; use strip/dot

antipatterns.name: Bidirectional (diverging) scale for unidirectional data
antipatterns.fix: use a sequential scale; endpoints map to real max/min/zero

antipatterns.name: Bar-plot meadow (many bars, multifactor)
antipatterns.fix: facet/group by the factor of interest

antipatterns.name: Not checking data range per factor level
antipatterns.fix: check per-factor range; narrow effects hide on a shared scale

antipatterns.name: Network graphs with a single layout
Confusing position-based vs length-based encodings
antipatterns.fix: bars must be zero-based (length); dots/box are position-based; no broken-axis bars

antipatterns.name: Concentric donuts
antipatterns.fix: unwrap to stacked bars (outer rings exaggerate)

antipatterns.name: Mixing stacked bars with mean separation
Boxplot for bimodal data
antipatterns.fix: show the distribution (raincloud/histogram/violin at large n)

okabe_ito.note: skill default; accent orange = key finding, blue = comparator, grey = control

prism_colorblind_safe.note: GraphPad-Prism look, CB-safe; good for biomedical audiences

npg.note: Nature journals; NOT deutan-safe (red #E64B35 vs green #00A087) — only with position+label+shape redundancy, else prefer okabe_ito

aaas.note: Science; red/green present — verify CB-safety or add redundancy

nejm.note: NEJM; verify CB-safety or add redundancy

lancet.note: Lancet; verify CB-safety or add redundancy

jama.note: JAMA; muted, differ in lightness — safer than most journal cycles but verify + add redundancy

scienceplots_nature.caveat: base 'science' needs LaTeX; the 'no-latex' overlay removes that dependency

scienceplots_science.caveat: drop 'no-latex' only if TeX Live is installed

compare_two_groups.method: test normality+equal-variance first; t-test if met else Mann-Whitney/Welch; report effect size + 95% CI, not just p

compare_multi_groups.method: one-way ANOVA + Tukey/Dunnett with multiplicity correction; name the test per panel

dose_response.method: fit model; report EC50/IC50 with CI + goodness

survival.method: Kaplan-Meier + log-rank; never an endpoint bar

agreement_pred_obs.method: parity (S13) + Bland-Altman (S14) + %PE / relative-L2 (domain); not bare R²

regression.method: report R2adj + residual plot + CI band; check heteroscedasticity

model_selection.method: AIC/AICc/BIC and/or cross-validation over ranked candidates; not raw R²

dimensionality.method: SVD/PCA scree + variance-explained at r + reconstruction error

PK_exposure.method: geometric mean, log-transform AUC/Cmax, 90% CI; BE = 80-125% of GMR CI

decision_rules.enforce: bare bars hide the sample -> use dot-on-bar (S1) or strip (S2); AP1
REJECT pie/donut; stacked or side-by-side bar (S24)
facet/small-multiples (M7) instead of a bar meadow
bars zero-based, no broken axis (length encodes value)
REJECT mean±SD-linear + t-test; geometric mean, log, 90% CI (S30/S25)
profile (S29) + f2/bootstrap-f2/Mahalanobis + %RSD; not a single-endpoint bar + ANOVA

decision_rules.when: a journal palette is requested but colorblind_safe==false
decision_rules.enforce: WARN; keep okabe_ito default OR add redundant encoding (position+direct label+marker/shape)
